steps.title: Read the evidence and the logical gap
steps.content: The passage establishes two data points: rocks from 4.02 bya resemble plume-setting rocks; rocks from 3.75 bya and 3.6 bya resemble subduction-setting rocks. The conclusion must follow strictly from this: subduction-like processes were already occurring by 3.75 bya (the earliest subduction-resembling sample), but not necessarily before 4.02 bya (which still showed plume characteristics).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — subduction-like processes began occurring in some locations no later than 3.75 bya.: Matches the evidence exactly — 3.75 bya is the earliest date of subduction-type rocks, so subduction must have started by then. ✓
  • Choice B — subduction replaced mantle plume formation as the most common geological process by about 4.02 bya.: Contradicts the data: 4.02 bya rocks still resemble plume-setting rocks, so subduction had not yet taken over by that date. ✗
  • Choice C — the majority of the rocks in the Acasta Gneiss Complex formed through subduction.: The passage never counts what proportion of rocks formed through subduction; this is an unsupported overreach. ✗
  • Choice D — the rocks in the Acasta Gneiss Complex are of a more recent origin than scientists previously thought.: The passage says nothing about revising the age of these rocks; this claim has no basis in the text. ✗
A is correct.
